subject: Add driver for Namco GunCon 2
author: Nolan Nicholson

impact.effect_if_absent: Namco GunCon 2 USB lightgun will not enumerate as an input device; Main_MiSTer lightgun calibration and quirk setup will be skipped for this device

userspace_coupling.interface: USB device enumeration (VID:PID detection); lightgun calibration and quirk initialization via input.cpp device detection
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: input.cpp:5361-5371 (GunCon 2 VID:PID 0x0b9a:0x016a detection, QUIRK_LIGHTGUN_CRT assignment, guncal[] initialization, input_lightgun_load() call)

license_provenance: GPL-2.0; copyright beardypig; based on PXRC driver by Marcus Folkesson
notes: Kernel driver is essential HID input driver for USB lightgun enumeration. Not in vanilla 6.18.38 (verified: no hid-guncon2.c, no USB_VENDOR_ID_NAMCO/USB_PRODUCT_ID_NAMCO_GUNCON2 in drivers/hid/hid-ids.h). Carried as 0010-hid-guncon2.patch (ported from 5.15 to 6.18.38, 2026-07-12); re-ported with style fixes (// comments → /* */, brace placement). Userspace coupling confirmed: Main_MiSTer explicitly detects VID:PID 0x0b9a:0x016a and sets QUIRK_LIGHTGUN_CRT + guncal[] calibration values (X_MIN=145, X_MAX=700, Y_MIN=25, Y_MAX=245 from driver defaults). Failure mode is silent: without this driver, device detection in input.cpp fails and lightgun feature silently unavailable. CONFIG_HID_GUNCON2=y in our linux.config (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config). Defconfig change is cross-architecture (arch/arm/configs/MiSTer_defconfig only — MiSTer is ARM-only platform). No soft dependency on other patches.
